title: Inside the paradox of Mexico's never-ending war on drugs
excerpt: An exploration of Mexico's decades-long struggle against drug cartels, examining why escalation has paradoxically deepened the crisis rather than resolving it.

title: Cigarettes after tax: The post-February crisis, and how smokers are reacting
excerpt: Following the Union Budget's steep tax hike on cigarettes, a look at the shifting habits of Indian smokers and the informal economies springing up in response.

title: Do jism, ek jaan? No thanks: How Gamophobia is changing the language of love
excerpt: A growing number of young Indians are choosing to stay unmarried, driven by a fear of commitment that is reshaping relationships and cultural expectations.

title: Beyond patriarchy: How the 'duty' men created became their greatest source of isolation
excerpt: An examination of how traditional masculine roles of provider and protector have left many men emotionally isolated, unable to seek help or express vulnerability.

title: A dead man's archive: What the Epstein files unleashed in 2025
excerpt: The release of sealed Epstein documents sent shockwaves through political and financial circles, reigniting demands for accountability years after his death.

title: Between two Indias: Life on the 2025 feed
excerpt: Social media in 2025 presents two starkly different versions of India — one aspirational, the other struggling — and the dissonance is shaping public discourse.

title: AI in classrooms: Helping students learn or doing the thinking for them?
excerpt: As AI tools become ubiquitous in Indian schools, educators and parents grapple with whether the technology enhances learning or simply replaces critical thought.

title: Wait… do I really need that bag, or is Instagram gaslighting me again?
excerpt: A reflective piece on how algorithmic feeds and influencer culture drive compulsive consumption, and the growing push among young consumers to resist the cycle.

title: An open letter from Gen Z: Are we really the problem or just the mirror no one wants to look into?
excerpt: A first-person defence of Generation Z, arguing that criticism directed at the cohort often reflects older generations' discomfort with changing social norms.
